title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · System Lens: Terminal Evidence Record
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux terminal evidence record increment in a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
Capture current user, directory, platform, environment, and relevant state before making a bounded change.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Terminal Evidence Record
steps.instruction:
theory case — On a theory host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Terminal Evidence Record contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Terminal Evidence Record while producing a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: On a worked example host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence. A defensible solution applies Terminal Evidence Record, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-terminal-evidence-record — Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
pwd
printf 'shell=%s\n' "$BASH_VERSION"
type -a printf
command -V pwd
printf 'status=%d\n' "$?"
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
